executiveSummary: All three user stories (US-001, US-002, US-003) and all nine functional requirements (FR-1 through FR-9) of iteration 000029 are implemented and verified in the codebase. The recording feature is complete: a Record/Stop button in App.tsx captures the R3F canvas and video-element audio via mediabunny, with full codec pre-flight checks, a live recording indicator, a finalizing spinner, and a session-persistent queue of downloadable MP4 entries. One minor deviation was found in FR-7: the codec pre-flight helpers are called with a plain codec string ('avc'/'aac') instead of the object form { codec, bitrate } specified in the PRD, which may reduce pre-flight accuracy for bitrate-specific support.

verificationByFR.notes: mediabunny v1.39.2 is listed in package.json dependencies.
Record/Stop buttons rendered directly in App.tsx (lines ~1455-1488). Record button disabled when !activeVideoUrl || isFinalizing.
Canvas obtained via gl.domElement inside visual-scene.tsx onCreated callback; propagated to App.tsx via handleCanvasCreated, stored in canvasRef.

Single AudioContext created and reused; video element piped through createMediaElementSource → createMediaStreamDestination; audio stays audible via connect(audioContext.destination) while also captured.
new Output({ format: new Mp4OutputFormat(), target: new BufferTarget() }) used; fastStart not explicitly set (library default applies).
canvas.captureStream(30) at 30 fps; video codec bitrate 4_000_000 (4 Mbps); audio codec bitrate 128_000 (128 kbps).

canEncodeVideo and canEncodeAudio are called with a plain codec string ('avc'/'aac') rather than the object form { codec: 'avc', bitrate: 4e6 } specified in the PRD. User-visible error is correctly shown on failure, but the pre-flight does not verify bitrate-specific encoding support.
Record button is replaced by Stop button while isRecording is true, preventing a second session. startRecording also cancels any lingering previous output object before starting.
RecordingQueue section renders each entry with filename, sizeInMb, and a native <a download> anchor element.

verificationByUS.notes: All ACs met. Record button visible/enabled when activeVideoUrl is set; onClick calls handleRecord() which seeks to 0 then calls startRecording(); mediabunny Output created with correct format, tracks, codecs, and bitrates; output.start() called after both tracks added; codec pre-flight with user-visible error; red pulsing dot + 'Recording…' label shown while isRecording.

All ACs met. video.onended fires stopRecording(); Stop button triggers video.pause() + stopRecording(); finalizing spinner (animate-spin, aria-live) replaces recording dot while isFinalizing; Record button hidden and replaced by Stop during active recording.

All ACs met. onFinalized wraps ArrayBuffer in Blob({ type: 'video/mp4' }), creates object URL, builds entry with recording-<ISO>.mp4 filename and MB size, appends to recordingEntries array. Download via <a href download> pattern. Each recording gets a unique incrementing ID. Entries persist for the session via React state.

minorObservations: The PRD describes an <audio> element as the playback source, but the implementation uses a <video> element (videoPlaybackRef). This is a valid adaptation since the app's media files carry both video and audio; functionally equivalent for audio capture purposes.

FR-7: canEncodeVideo/canEncodeAudio are invoked with a plain string argument ('avc'/'aac') rather than the { codec, bitrate } object form specified in the PRD. This means the pre-flight does not verify that the specific target bitrate is supported, only that the codec itself is encodable.
The Record button is also disabled during isFinalizing (not just when no activeVideoUrl). This is a sensible addition beyond the PRD requirement that prevents double-starting.
No explicit UI feedback is shown when recording starts from the beginning of the video (seek reset happens silently). A brief toast or status message could improve clarity.

Object URLs created for queue entries are never revoked (URL.revokeObjectURL) during the session. This is acceptable for a session-scoped feature but will hold Blob memory for the entire page lifetime.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: The iteration 000029 implementation fully satisfies its stated goals. All user stories comply and 8 of 9 functional requirements fully comply; FR-7 partially complies due to the missing bitrate argument in the codec pre-flight calls. Recommended action: update canEncodeVideo and canEncodeAudio calls in use-recorder.ts to pass the full options object ({ codec: 'avc', bitrate: 4e6 } and { codec: 'aac', bitrate: 128e3 }) to align with the PRD spec and improve pre-flight accuracy. Optionally, add URL.revokeObjectURL cleanup when queue entries are removed to avoid long-lived memory leaks.
